summary: Descriptive overview of intentionaltorts in substantive law: battery, assault, false imprisonment, intentional infliction.
key_points: battery is a fundamental concept in tort law.
Legal standards for intentionaltorts are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine intentionaltorts in litigation.
The doctrine of intentionaltorts has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding intentionaltorts requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
